response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled software craftsman with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and web/distributed systems, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, hands-on Spring/enterprise framework experience, and genuine interest in IDE internals and JVM tooling — none of which are evident in his resume. His Java exposure is peripheral and dated (robotic arms at Altom in 2016–2018, C IDE work at NI in 2014–2015 in C/C++), and there is no mention of Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any JVM-centric development at meaningful scale. His most recent work has moved further from the JVM, not closer.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applicable to any engineering environment
Experience with developer tooling and MCP/agentic frameworks at re:cinq shows genuine interest in the tooling space
Exposure to large-scale, high-throughput systems (Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests/second) demonstrates systems-level thinking
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin experience — primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js with only incidental and outdated Java usage
Zero evidence of Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or any enterprise JVM framework knowledge
No background in IDE internals, language tooling, or JVM-level development — the core technical requirement of this role
